title: nf-core/oceangenomesmitogenomes pipeline parameters
description: An nf-core pipeline for mitogenome assembly and LCA for species validation

input_output_options.title: Input/output options
input_output_options.description: Define where the pipeline should find input data and save output data.

input.description: Path to comma-separated file containing information about the samples in the experiment.
input.help_text: You will need to create a design file with information about the samples in your experiment before running the pipeline. Use this parameter to specify its location. It has to be a comma-separated file with 3 columns, and a header row. See [usage docs](https://nf-co.re/oceangenomesmitogenomes/usage#samplesheet-input).

input_dir.description: Path to FASTQ files using glob pattern

outdir.description: The output directory where the results will be saved. You have to use absolute paths to storage on Cloud infrastructure.

email.help_text: Set this parameter to your e-mail address to get a summary e-mail with details of the run sent to you when the workflow exits. If set in your user config file (`~/.nextflow/config`) then you don't need to specify this on the command line for every run.

multiqc_title.description: MultiQC report title. Printed as page header, used for filename if not otherwise specified.

institutional_config_options.title: Institutional config options
institutional_config_options.description: Parameters used to describe centralised config profiles. These should not be edited.
institutional_config_options.help_text: The centralised nf-core configuration profiles use a handful of pipeline parameters to describe themselves. This information is then printed to the Nextflow log when you run a pipeline. You should not need to change these values when you run a pipeline.

custom_config_version.description: Git commit id for Institutional configs.

custom_config_base.help_text: If you're running offline, Nextflow will not be able to fetch the institutional config files from the internet. If you don't need them, then this is not a problem. If you do need them, you should download the files from the repo and tell Nextflow where to find them with this parameter.

generic_options.title: Generic options
generic_options.description: Less common options for the pipeline, typically set in a config file.
generic_options.help_text:
These options are common to all nf-core pipelines and allow you to customise some of the core preferences for how the pipeline runs.

Typically these options would be set in a Nextflow config file loaded for all pipeline runs, such as `~/.nextflow/config`.

publish_dir_mode.description: Method used to save pipeline results to output directory.
publish_dir_mode.help_text: The Nextflow `publishDir` option specifies which intermediate files should be saved to the output directory. This option tells the pipeline what method should be used to move these files. See [Nextflow docs](https://www.nextflow.io/docs/latest/process.html#publishdir) for details.

email_on_fail.description: Email address for completion summary, only when pipeline fails.
email_on_fail.help_text: An email address to send a summary email to when the pipeline is completed - ONLY sent if the pipeline does not exit successfully.

plaintext_email.description: Send plain-text email instead of HTML.

max_multiqc_email_size.description: File size limit when attaching MultiQC reports to summary emails.

monochrome_logs.description: Do not use coloured log outputs.

hook_url.description: Incoming hook URL for messaging service
hook_url.help_text: Incoming hook URL for messaging service. Currently, MS Teams and Slack are supported.

multiqc_config.description: Custom config file to supply to MultiQC.

multiqc_logo.description: Custom logo file to supply to MultiQC. File name must also be set in the MultiQC config file

skip_per_sample_multiqc.description: Skip generation of one MultiQC report per sample.

multiqc_methods_description.description: Custom MultiQC yaml file containing HTML including a methods description.

validate_params.description: Boolean whether to validate parameters against the schema at runtime

pipelines_testdata_base_path.description: Base URL or local path to location of pipeline test dataset files

trace_report_suffix.description: Suffix to add to the trace report filename. Default is the date and time in the format yyyy-MM-dd_HH-mm-ss.

organelle_type.description: Organelle label passed to GetOrganelle (e.g. animal_mt).

blast_db_dir.description: Directory used to cache downloaded BLAST taxdb files.

taxonkit_db_dir.description: Directory used to cache the NCBI taxdump for TaxonKit.

curated_blast_db.description: Path to curated BLAST database fasta.

nt_blast_db.description: Path to NCBI nt BLAST database for invertebrate samples.

mitos_refdb.description: Path to the MITOS2 RefSeq reference data directory (parent of the version dir), downloaded from Zenodo. Required for invertebrate (MITOS2) annotation.

mitos_refseq_ver.description: MITOS2 reference version subdir name passed to runmitos.py -r (e.g. refseq89m for RefSeq89 Metazoa).

template_sbt.description: GenBank submission template passed to table2asn.

ena_webin_validate.description: Validate passing ENA EMBL flat files with Webin-CLI. Requires ena_study and the WEBIN_USERNAME/WEBIN_PASSWORD Nextflow secrets.

ena_study.description: ENA study accession or alias used in per-sample targeted-sequence manifests.

ena_mode.description: Mode for the standalone ena.nf runner: convert table2asn GBF files then validate, or validate existing EMBL files only.

ena_input.description: Mode-specific CSV input for the standalone ena.nf runner.

ena_validation_attempt.description: Cache key for Webin validation. Change it to deliberately revalidate unchanged files when using -resume.

sql_config.description: Database config file for SQL connection.

tempdir.description: Temporary directory for large intermediate files.

kvalue.description: K-mer size for related genome modules.

translation_table.description: Mitochondrial genetic code for vertebrate/unresolved samples. Invertebrate codes are derived per-sample from taxonomic class.

mitogenome_summary_min_mean_coverage.description: Minimum mean mitogenome assembly coverage before the MultiQC assembly summary flags low_mean_coverage.

mitogenome_summary_max_coverage_cv.description: Maximum allowed coverage coefficient of variation before the MultiQC assembly summary flags high_coverage_variability.

mitogenome_summary_min_length.description: Minimum expected mitogenome assembly length in bp for the MultiQC assembly summary.

mitogenome_summary_max_length.description: Maximum expected mitogenome assembly length in bp for the MultiQC assembly summary.

mitogenome_summary_expected_gene_count.description: Expected mitochondrial gene count used when annotation-derived gene counts are available.

mitogenome_summary_expected_pcg_count.description: Expected mitochondrial protein-coding gene (CDS) count. The summary flags missing_protein_coding_genes when the annotation-derived CDS count falls below this, catching collapses that tRNAs mask in the total gene count.

max_cpus.description: Maximum CPUs to use for processes.

refreshModules.description: Force module cache refresh when running on HPC systems.

getorganelle_fromreads_args.description: Override the default GetOrganelle from-reads arguments (default '-R 20 -w 95 --continue').

getorganelle_genedb_min_genes.description: Minimum number of genes a reference must yield to build the reseed custom gene (label) database. References below this keep the first-pass GetOrganelle assembly instead of reseeding.

hic_fastp_args.description: Override the fastp arguments used to trim raw Hi-C reads before GetOrganelle.

force_db_overwrite.description: Overwrite existing mitogenome_data rows on SQL upload. By default the PUSH_* modules are insert-only and preserve existing rows.

mitohifi_container.description: MitoHiFi container image. The version embedded in mt_assembly_prefix and versions.yml is parsed from this tag.

enable_oatk_fallback.description: Enable the reference-free Oatk HiFi fallback. Runs only on samples MitoHiFi failed to assemble (no close NCBI reference). Requires oatk_mito_db when enabled.

oatk_mito_db.description: Path to the OatkDB <clade>_mito.fam profile-HMM (with its .h3f/.h3i/.h3m/.h3p index files staged beside it). Required when enable_oatk_fallback is true. Fetch with bin/download_oatk_db.sh.

oatk_container.description: Container image bundling oatk, syncasm and nhmmscan for the Oatk fallback.

oatk_syncmer_coverage.description: Syncmer coverage threshold (-c) passed to Oatk/syncasm.

skip_and_reuse_options.title: Skip and reuse options
skip_and_reuse_options.description: Skip pipeline stages and reuse precomputed artefacts for staged re-runs.

skip_getorganelle_reseed.description: Skip the automatic GetOrganelle reseed pass on failed/fragmented first-pass assemblies.

skip_upload_results.description: Skip SQL upload modules and QC gating.

skip_hic_fastp.description: Skip fastp trimming of raw Hi-C reads before GetOrganelle.

precomputed_mitogenome_assembly_fasta_ge.description: Glob of precomputed GetOrganelle assembly FASTA files used when the GetOrganelle branch is skipped.

precomputed_mitogenome_assembly_log_geto.description: Glob of precomputed GetOrganelle logs used when the GetOrganelle branch is skipped.

precomputed_mitogenome_circularity_evide.description: Glob of precomputed GetOrganelle circularity-check TSVs (*.getorg_check.tsv) used when the GetOrganelle branch is skipped, so meta.circular is still resolved from the original run's verdict instead of defaulting to unknown/linear.

precomputed_mitogenome_assembly_fasta_hi.description: Glob of precomputed MitoHiFi assembly FASTA files used when the HiFi branch is skipped.

precomputed_mitogenome_assembly_log_hifi.description: Glob of precomputed MitoHiFi logs used when the HiFi branch is skipped.

precomputed_mitogenome_circularity_evide.description: Glob of precomputed MitoHiFi circularity-check TSVs (*.circularity_check.tsv) used when the HiFi branch is skipped, so meta.circular is still resolved from the original run's verdict instead of defaulting to unknown/linear.

precomputed_mitogenome_annotation_result.description: Glob of precomputed annotation files (fasta, gff, tbl/gb) used when annotation is skipped. FORMAT_FILES needs the whole per-assembly bundle, not just the GFF.

precomputed_mitogenome_blast_results.description: Glob of precomputed filtered BLAST TSV files used when annotation is skipped.

precomputed_mitogenome_lca_results.description: Glob of precomputed LCA TSV files used when annotation is skipped.

precomputed_mitogenome_lca_raw_results.description: Glob of precomputed raw LCA TSV files used when annotation is skipped.
